Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a scenario of an order fulfillment process with 10 activities We need to define constraints based on the typical flow of the process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Steps 1 Receive Order RO The process starts here 2 Check Availability CA After receiving we check if items are available 3 Reserve Items RI If available we reserve the items 4 Pick Items PI Then we pick the items 5 Quality Check QC After picking we check the quality 6 Pack Order PO Then we pack the order 7 Generate Shipping Label GSL Then we generate the shipping label 8 Dispatch D Then we dispatch the package

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Invoice Customer IC We also need to invoice the customer But note when Typically invoicing might happen after the order is confirmed maybe after reservation or after picking but before shipping However the problem says once the customer pays for Record Payment but invoicing comes before payment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets assume a typical flow RO to CA to RI to PI to QC to PO to GSL to D And then separately after RI or after RO but after the order is confirmed we might have RO to CA to RI to IC to RP

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However the problem does not specify the exact timing of IC and RP relative to the physical fulfillment But we can assume that invoicing happens after the order is confirmed so after RI

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Possibility 1 Payment is required before picking so RP must be before PI Possibility 2 Payment can be after picking but before dispatch so RP must be before D but can be after PI

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Since the problem does not specify we can avoid defining an order between say IC and PI But then we have two independent sequences after RI

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can define Always Before constraints for the physical chain RO before CA CA before RI RI before PI PI before QC QC before PO PO before GSL GSL before D Always Before constraints for the financial chain RO before CA CA before RI RI before IC IC before RP

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The physical chain RO to CA to RI to PI to QC to PO to GSL to D And the financial chain RI to IC to RP

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Given the ambiguity we will model the physical chain and the financial chain as two separate sequences that both start after RI

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before if an activity occurs then its predecessors must have occurred For example for CA we want to enforce that if CA occurs then RO must have occurred so we put CA RO in always before

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always After if RO occurs then CA must occur after RO Then CA RI Then RI PI Then RI IC Then PI QC Then QC PO Then PO GSL Then GSL D Then IC RP

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But we have a potential issue the constraint IC RP in always after forces that if IC occurs then RP must occur But the customer might not pay So we cannot enforce that

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Therefore we change Remove IC RP from always after

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: How to model skipping We set activ freq for each activity 0 or 1 Then we have to adjust the always constraints they are conditional on the occurrence of the first activity

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Given the complexity and the fact that the problem is ambiguous and since the initial instructions did not specify whether activities can be skipped we will assume that the process is followed without skipping

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The final decision We assume every activity occurs exactly once We assume the process is followed without skipping We assume that invoicing and payment are part of the process and payment is recorded We assume that the financial chain is RI to IC to RP and the physical chain is RI to PI to QC to PO to GSL to D and the two chains are interleaved arbitrarily after RI

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Then the constraints are as in the first version

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: log skeleton equivalence set always before CA RO RI CA PI RI IC RI QC PI PO QC GSL PO D GSL RP IC always after RO CA CA RI RI PI RI IC PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D IC RP never together set activ freq RO 1 CA 1 RI 1 PI 1 QC 1 PO 1 GSL 1 D 1 IC 1 RP 1 directly follows set
